text:
A sleepy squirrel tiptoed toward the chestnut oak's heart. It pecked at ripe acorns. There was a pitter-patter next to it. A wise old owl hooted from somewhere above.

The squirrel glanced down and saw a tiny bat fluttering nearby. "Hello there," it said. "You don't look very sleepy."

The bat hooted back. "Oh, I could sleep forever."

The squirrel couldn't help smiling. "How about a nap next to me? I could do without forever."

The bat blinked, then flew closer. "Alright. But just don't bother me with dreams of acorns."

As they settled in the bark, the squirrel looked skyward. "Sometimes I think I've seen you dream of golden nuts."

The bat hooted softly. "Only sometimes. Otherwise, I'm in my cave."

"The forest's busy today. You hear all sorts of things."

"It's okay. I've heard yours."
